transcript: This is the picture. Just tell me what's happening in the picture. The boy is slipping off the stool. He's trying to steal cookies. The mother is working at the sink with the water running over. She's. . . The little girl is saying "Shh" so the mother don't hear. Did I say the sink was running over? Okay. Uh-huh, you did. Mother's drying the dishes. I don't see anything else. Okay, that's fine.
patient_only_transcript: The boy is slipping off the stool. He's trying to steal cookies. The mother is working at the sink with the water running over. The little girl is saying 'Shh' so the mother don't hear. Mother's drying the dishes. I don't see anything else.
speaker_turn_summary: Examiner: 'This is the picture. Just tell me what's happening in the picture.'

Patient: 'The boy is slipping off the stool. He's trying to steal cookies. The mother is working at the sink with the water running over. The little girl is saying 'Shh' so the mother don't hear. Mother's drying the dishes. I don't see anything else.'
Examiner: 'Did I say the sink was running over?'
patient_cue_summary: Coherent scene description with correct key elements (boy, stool, cookies, mother, sink, girl, 'Shh')
Minimal examiner dependence — patient initiates and completes full description without prompting
No obvious word-finding pauses, circumlocution, or semantic vagueness
No repetition, self-correction, or abandoned utterances
Narrative is logically sequenced and causally linked (girl says 'Shh' to prevent mother from hearing)
Patient ends with 'I don't see anything else' — neutral, not indicative of cognitive deficit

content_findings.label: Scene Description Coherence
content_findings.summary: Patient accurately and sequentially describes the entire picture scene, including characters, actions, and causal relationships.
content_findings.evidence: Patient states: 'The boy is slipping off the stool. He's trying to steal cookies. The mother is working at the sink with the water running over. The little girl is saying 'Shh' so the mother don't hear. Mother's drying the dishes. I don't see anything else.'

content_findings.label: Lexical Retrieval and Specificity
content_findings.summary: Patient uses specific, contextually appropriate terms without vagueness or circumlocution.
content_findings.evidence: Patient names specific objects (cookies, sink, stool, dishes) and actions (slipping, stealing, drying, saying 'Shh').

content_findings.label: Narrative Organization
content_findings.summary: Patient organizes the description logically, linking events causally (girl says 'Shh' to prevent mother from hearing).
content_findings.evidence: Patient connects the girl’s action to the mother’s unawareness, indicating understanding of narrative causality.

assessment.screening_summary: The patient provides a coherent, complete, and logically organized description of the pictured scene, accurately identifying all major actors and actions. There is no evidence of word-finding difficulty, disorganization, or examiner dependence. The patient’s speech is clear, informative, and task-relevant. No impairment-like cues are present.
evidence_against_risk: Accurate recall of multiple scene elements
Self-contained description without reliance on examiner prompts
alternative_explanations: The patient may be a child or young adult with good language and memory skills
The task may be within the patient’s cognitive capacity
The patient may be describing a familiar or rehearsed scenario
risk_control_suggestions: No further cognitive screening indicated based on this clip
Continue routine observation if part of a longitudinal assessment
assessment.future_risk_trend_summary: Longitudinal prediction is not supported by a single clip; no evidence of cognitive decline is present.
context_notes: assessment is based solely on audio.
Patient speech is intelligible and task-relevant
Examiner prompts are minimal and do not dominate the interaction.
assessment.disclaimer: Research demo only. This output is not a diagnosis and must not be used as a standalone medical decision.
